customers.name: Everyday Member
customers.description: An individual using Cadre directly or through an employer-sponsored plan to find timely, relatable support for everyday mental wellbeing challenges such as stress, relationships, caregiving, work pressure, grief, neurodiversity, or life transitions. Success means feeling safe enough to engage quickly, finding relevant live or on-demand support, and building a repeatable habit without stigma.
careAbout: Getting support quickly without a long intake process
Feeling anonymous and psychologically safe while engaging
Finding topics and people that feel relatable instead of clinical or generic
Being able to join live or on-demand support on mobile or web
Receiving practical reminders and recommendations that actually fit current needs
winsThem: Fast personalized support discovery
Daily live sessions and active communities
Vetted contributors and trusted resources
Privacy-friendly participation and clear consent handling
Progress cues that reinforce return usage without pressure
theirFear: Being exposed, judged, or identified when asking for help
Wasting time on generic content that does not match the current problem
Joining a community that feels unsafe, unmoderated, or emotionally draining

jobsToBeDone.name: Find meaningful support in the moment of need
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Turn a difficult moment into a quick next step by finding a live session, trusted resource, community conversation, or guided recommendation that feels relevant right now.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Lower time-to-support, higher confidence that the platform is helpful, and reduced abandonment in early sessions.

steps.step: Describe or infer the current need
steps.description: Start from a topic, feeling, life situation, or prompt and quickly narrow the field without needing a long assessment.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Member Home and Intent Feed
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Builds a first screen that reflects current topic, recent behavior, and likely urgency.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Topic and Pathway Taxonomy
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Maps messy wellbeing concerns into relatable topics and entry paths.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Support Recommendations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Suggests the most relevant next support action based on context and history.

steps.description: Assess whether a session, community, contributor, or resource feels safe and relevant enough to join.

capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Shows why a contributor is credible, relatable, and appropriate for the topic.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Community Feed
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Lets members evaluate the tone and usefulness of current conversations.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Privacy, Consent, and Data Separation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Reassures members that participation is confidential and employer-safe.

steps.description: Enter the chosen experience quickly and continue into the next helpful action afterward.

capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Enables immediate participation in daily live sessions and event-based support.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Resource Directory and Care Navigation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides trusted next steps when community support alone is not enough.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Notifications and Reminders
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps the member connected to the next relevant support touchpoint.

jobsToBeDone.name: Build a sustainable wellbeing habit without stigma
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Turn one-time relief into a lightweight ongoing routine of community participation, helpful content, and personal progress reflection.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher repeat engagement, stronger perceived value, and better retention over time.

steps.description: Save relevant topics, follow communities, and choose reminders that fit life patterns instead of creating more pressure.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Topic Communities and Circles
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Creates repeat entry points around issues that matter to the member.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Progress and Reflection Tracking
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Shows simple progress markers, streaks, and reflections that make ongoing effort feel visible.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Notifications and Reminders
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Delivers timely prompts that keep habits active without overwhelming the member.

steps.description: Return for live sessions, community conversations, or saved content that reinforces social support and learning.

capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps the member connected to active conversations and peer support.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Live Q&A and Ask-a-Question
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Lets members ask targeted questions and receive timely responses.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: In-Platform Messaging and Follow-Up
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports guided follow-up and continuity after meaningful interactions.

steps.description: Move from self-guided engagement toward higher-touch support or outside care when risk or need increases.

customerJourneyStories.name: From a stressful moment to a repeat support routine
customerJourneyStories.summary: A member arrives with a real-life mental wellbeing concern, finds a safe entry point quickly, joins support that feels relatable, and then builds a repeatable habit around community, reminders, and trusted resources.

stages.narrative: A stressful work, family, grief, or life-transition moment pushes the member to look for support now rather than later.

media.title: Everyday Member journey stage 1: Trigger
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 1 of "From a stressful moment to a repeat support routine": Trigger.

stages.narrative: The member expects Cadre to narrow the field quickly into relatable topics, sessions, and communities.

media.title: Everyday Member journey stage 2: Discovery
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 2 of "From a stressful moment to a repeat support routine": Discovery.

stages.narrative: Trust depends on contributor credibility, community tone, privacy confidence, and clarity about what happens next.

media.title: Everyday Member journey stage 3: Evaluation
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 3 of "From a stressful moment to a repeat support routine": Evaluation.

stages.narrative: The member joins a live or on-demand experience and tests whether it feels supportive enough to return.

media.title: Everyday Member journey stage 4: Trial
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 4 of "From a stressful moment to a repeat support routine": Trial.

stages.narrative: Cadre becomes valuable when the member can come back without friction and find the right next action repeatedly.

media.title: Everyday Member journey stage 5: Engagement
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 5 of "From a stressful moment to a repeat support routine": Engagement.

stages.narrative: Retention grows when the member feels less alone, sees progress, and trusts Cadre as an ongoing support companion.

media.title: Everyday Member journey stage 6: Retention
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 6 of "From a stressful moment to a repeat support routine": Retention.

top.name: Weekly meaningful support actions
top.description: Average number of live joins, saved resources, guided reflections, or meaningful community interactions per weekly active member.

children.name: Time to first meaningful support interaction
children.description: Median time from session start to joining a live session, saving a resource, or starting a guided activity.

children.name: Recommendation acceptance rate
children.description: Share of recommendation impressions that lead to a meaningful member action within the same day.

children.name: Privacy confidence score
children.description: Share of surveyed members who agree that Cadre feels private and safe enough for honest participation.

children.name: 30-day member retention
children.description: Share of newly activated members who return for a meaningful support action within 30 days.

top.name: Paid member gross retention
top.description: Share of paying or sponsored active members retained after 90 days.

children.name: Free-to-paid conversion
children.description: Share of free members who convert to an individual or employer-sponsored paid state within 60 days.

children.name: Average monthly revenue per paying member
children.description: Average recognized revenue per active paying or sponsored member.

children.name: Safety incident rate
children.description: Share of weekly active members associated with escalated safety incidents.

children.name: Content completion rate
children.description: Share of started guided experiences or recorded sessions completed past the defined threshold.

productStrategy.vision: Make Cadre the default everyday support system people trust when life becomes emotionally hard, not only when they need formal care.

1_year.focus: Reduce time-to-support and improve weekly habit strength for members arriving through both direct and employer channels.
1_year.productTheme: Fast, safe, and relatable support in the flow of daily life.

supporting: Time to first meaningful support interaction

milestones: Launch stronger topic-to-support routing
Improve personalized reminders and saved pathways
Standardize care-navigation escalation triggers

3_year.focus: Turn Cadre into a durable support network that blends community, contributor guidance, and care navigation with clearer personalization.
3_year.productTheme: Personalized social support with trusted escalation paths.

businessKPI.northStar: Net revenue retention from paying member cohorts

milestones: Unify support recommendations across live, community, and resources
Add richer guided reflection and recovery pathways
Scale multilingual and life-stage topic coverage

5_year.focus: Become a mainstream mental wellbeing participation layer that employers and individuals trust as an always-available companion to better care and culture.
milestones: Establish Cadre as an employer-standard mental wellbeing layer
Expand trusted partner and care-navigation ecosystem
Operationalize longitudinal wellbeing journeys with strong privacy controls

customers.name: People and Benefits Leader
customers.description: A CHRO, people leader, or benefits buyer evaluating Cadre as a mental wellbeing benefit for a workforce. Success means deploying a benefit that activates quickly, feels meaningfully different from a low-use EAP, supports culture and retention goals, and renews without heavy operational overhead.
careAbout: A clear employee need addressed better than status quo EAP usage
Fast rollout with low procurement and implementation friction
Meaningful engagement after launch rather than vanity enrollment
Safe employer boundaries around confidentiality and data handling
Evidence that the program helps culture, wellbeing, and retention goals
winsThem: Simple team pricing and low-per-seat economics
Strong member activation and usefulness rates
Employer-specific live events and launch support
Clear privacy separation between employer and member experience
Reporting that shows engagement and program momentum
theirFear: Buying another wellness benefit that employees ignore
Owning risk if privacy or emotional safety boundaries are unclear
Having no defensible renewal story after the first contract cycle

jobsToBeDone.name: Launch a credible mental wellbeing benefit quickly
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Move from need recognition and procurement to a live, employer-sponsored Cadre program with clear rollout materials and member access paths.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Lower implementation effort, faster launch, and higher confidence in first-quarter activation.

steps.description: Assess whether Cadre fits workforce needs, complements existing benefits, and addresses engagement gaps in current support models.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Team Plan Packaging and Contracting
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Clarifies team pricing, seat structure, and commercial packaging.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Employer Live Events Program
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Shows how Cadre can support culture moments and launch narratives beyond static benefits enrollment.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Employer Engagement Analytics
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides proof points for expected activation and value realization.

capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports activation campaigns and follow-up nudges.

jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Understand whether Cadre is activating employees, creating useful engagement, and supporting leadership goals well enough to justify renewal and expansion.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher renewal confidence and clearer expansion or program-adjustment decisions.

customerJourneyStories.summary: A benefits buyer chooses Cadre to increase real mental wellbeing engagement, launches it to the workforce, and then needs enough activation and usefulness evidence to renew with confidence.

stages.narrative: The employer sees low utilization or weak employee trust in existing mental health support options.

media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 4 of "From low-confidence wellness stack to defensible renewal": Trial.

productStrategy.vision: Make Cadre the employer mental wellbeing benefit that people leaders renew because employees actually use it and trust it.

1_year.focus: Improve launch readiness, workforce activation, and quarterly renewal confidence.
1_year.productTheme: Fast rollout with visible employee usefulness.
milestones: Standardize employer launch kits and access-code setup
Add better quarterly review reporting for buyers
Increase live-event attach on new contracts

3_year.focus: Become a standard layer in the employer wellbeing stack across distributed, deskless, and mid-market workforces.
3_year.productTheme: High-engagement mental wellbeing infrastructure for teams.

businessKPI.northStar: Net revenue retention for employer accounts

milestones: Package differentiated launch motions by workforce type
Expand employer analytics and benchmark views
Create repeatable annual programming calendars

5_year.focus: Anchor Cadre as a trusted social-wellbeing layer that employers use to complement clinical, leave, and people-development programs.

supporting: Benefit advocacy rate among people leaders

milestones: Establish Cadre as a category-defining alternative to low-use EAP engagement
Build partner integrations into broader employer wellbeing ecosystems
Operationalize scalable benchmarking for renewal and upsell

customers.name: HR Program Administrator
customers.description: An HR, benefits, or internal communications operator responsible for rolling out Cadre inside an organization, sustaining adoption, and coordinating campaigns or events. Success means keeping administration simple while maintaining workforce participation, confidentiality, and timely program adjustments.
careAbout: Low-overhead setup and user administration
Clear launch materials and access controls
Meaningful usage signals without exposing individual activity
Easy coordination of custom events and topic campaigns
Fast answers when employees or leaders have questions
winsThem: Simple org onboarding and access-code flows
Admin analytics that are clear and non-clinical
Event scheduling and communications support
Reliable member-facing product quality after launch
Explicit privacy boundaries for employer access
theirFear: Being pulled into support operations the product should handle
Launching a program that creates confusion about confidentiality
Not knowing how to intervene when engagement drops

jobsToBeDone.name: Operate the program without heavy administrative burden
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Configure employer access, coordinate launch communications, and keep the Cadre program running smoothly without becoming a full-time operations burden.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Lower admin effort and fewer avoidable launch or access failures.

steps.description: Set up eligible cohorts, access paths, admins, and launch assets while preserving privacy boundaries.

children.description: Share of flagged low-engagement accounts that recover to healthy participation within one quarter.

productStrategy.vision: Give employer operators a low-burden control plane for running Cadre well without forcing them into sensitive support work.

1_year.focus: Reduce admin effort and increase first-quarter program activation.
1_year.productTheme: Simple employer operations with clear privacy guardrails.

supporting: Admin support tickets per 100 enrolled employees

milestones: Ship clearer admin workflows and access-code management
Add actionable but privacy-safe engagement summaries
Reduce launch issue rate for new employer cohorts

3_year.focus: Let operators tailor Cadre programming to workforce segments without increasing compliance or operational risk.
3_year.productTheme: Adaptive employer operations for sustained participation.

supporting: Operational cost per active employer account

milestones: Add employer segmentation and benchmark views
Operationalize repeatable programming playbooks
Automate more proactive account health guidance

5_year.focus: Make Cadre employer operations mature enough to run across large, distributed, multi-segment organizations with low friction.
milestones: Support complex enterprise cohorting and governance
Create scalable benchmark and planning tooling
Lower program operations cost through guided automation

customers.name: Contributor / Facilitator
customers.description: A vetted expert, peer guide, speaker, or facilitator creating content, hosting live sessions, answering questions, or participating in employer events through Cadre. Success means reaching the right audience, operating safely and efficiently, and feeling that Cadre is a credible distribution and income channel.
careAbout: A trustworthy platform and audience fit
Simple content publishing and live-session workflows
Clear standards for moderation, safety, and role expectations
Reliable scheduling, attendance, and payout operations
Evidence that their work is resonating and helping people
winsThem: Strong contributor profile and credential presentation
Reliable livestream and Q&A tooling
Clear moderation and escalation policies
Useful audience engagement analytics
Predictable payouts and employer event opportunities
theirFear: Being put into unsafe or poorly moderated situations
Investing time into content that reaches the wrong audience or no audience
Working on a platform that feels operationally immature or hard to trust

jobsToBeDone.name: Publish and deliver trusted support experiences
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Create content, host sessions, or answer questions on Cadre in a way that feels safe, high-quality, and easy to operate.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher contributor productivity, stronger audience trust, and more consistent content supply.

steps.description: Set up profile, credentials, topic coverage, and participation rules so the audience knows why the contributor is relevant.

customerJourneyStories.name: From trusted onboarding to repeat contribution
customerJourneyStories.summary: A contributor joins Cadre because it offers a safer and more mission-aligned audience, then stays because the platform makes publishing, live sessions, demand matching, and payouts reliable enough to sustain participation.

stages.narrative: The contributor wants to reach people with mental wellbeing content or facilitation in a more trusted environment.

media.title: Contributor / Facilitator journey stage 1: Trigger
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 1 of "From trusted onboarding to repeat contribution": Trigger.

stages.narrative: Cadre stands out when contributor purpose, audience fit, and employer-event opportunity are visible.

media.title: Contributor / Facilitator journey stage 2: Discovery
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 2 of "From trusted onboarding to repeat contribution": Discovery.

stages.narrative: The contributor tests whether credibility, moderation, and operational maturity feel strong enough to trust.

media.title: Contributor / Facilitator journey stage 3: Evaluation
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 3 of "From trusted onboarding to repeat contribution": Evaluation.

stages.narrative: The first published content item or live session reveals whether the workflow is manageable and effective.

media.title: Contributor / Facilitator journey stage 4: Trial
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 4 of "From trusted onboarding to repeat contribution": Trial.

stages.narrative: The contributor becomes committed when opportunities, analytics, and payouts support a repeat practice.

media.title: Contributor / Facilitator journey stage 5: Engagement
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 5 of "From trusted onboarding to repeat contribution": Engagement.

stages.narrative: Long-term supply is retained when contributors feel safe, useful, and fairly rewarded.

media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 6 of "From trusted onboarding to repeat contribution": Retention.

top.name: Monthly meaningful audience interactions
top.description: Average number of live, Q&A, or on-demand interactions that meet Cadre's meaningful-engagement threshold per active contributor each month.

children.name: Contributor utilization
children.description: Share of active contributors delivering at least one scheduled session, content drop, or event each month.

children.name: Publishing cycle time
children.description: Median time from content submission to approved publication or scheduled live session.

children.name: Session attendee satisfaction
children.description: Share of surveyed attendees rating contributor-led sessions as useful or very useful.

children.name: Contributor safety confidence
children.description: Share of active contributors who agree Cadre provides clear moderation and escalation support.

top.name: Contributor retention rate
top.description: Share of active contributors retained across rolling two-quarter windows.

children.name: Contribution margin per delivered session
children.description: Margin remaining after contributor payout and direct delivery cost for live sessions.

children.name: Employer event fill rate
children.description: Share of requested employer event slots successfully staffed with an appropriate contributor.

children.name: Payout accuracy rate
children.description: Share of payout cycles completed without correction.

children.name: Moderation escalations resolved within SLA
children.description: Share of contributor-related safety escalations resolved inside the target service level.

productStrategy.vision: Make Cadre the contributor platform where trusted experts and facilitators can reliably create impact without operational chaos or safety ambiguity.

1_year.focus: Improve contributor onboarding, publishing speed, session quality, and payout trust.
1_year.productTheme: Trusted contributor operations at daily-delivery quality.
milestones: Tighten contributor onboarding and credential workflows
Improve live-session operations and moderation support
Reduce publishing cycle time and payout exceptions

3_year.focus: Turn Cadre into a durable contributor network with clear topic-market fit and efficient opportunity matching.
3_year.productTheme: Specialized supply network for social mental wellbeing support.
milestones: Build stronger contributor demand-matching logic
Create reusable program and content bundles
Expand contributor categories by life-stage and topic

5_year.focus: Create a scaled trusted contributor marketplace embedded in Cadre's support ecosystem while preserving quality and safety.
milestones: Scale the contributor network without weakening trust standards
Offer richer contributor operating tools and specialization paths
Standardize reusable premium support programming across segments
